page: Amazon Web Services i8g.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom ARMv9-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz on an arm64 architecture, with no hyperthreading. The processor configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the i8g.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Its multi-core CPU capabilities, measured via stress-ng, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression place it in the top 10% of tested systems, though single-core performance remains in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, achieving top-tier results for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. The system also excels in database operations, software compilation, and static web serving throughput, all ranking in the top tier. However, LLM inference performance is mixed, showing weak results for prompt processing on small models but strong results on medium models.

Given its high resource density, the i8g.48xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require massive local storage capacity paired with high memory bandwidth and dedicated compute resources. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and 45 TB of NVMe SSD make it highly suitable for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, and intensive data-processing tasks. The lack of hardware-accelerated GPUs makes it less suitable for heavy AI training, but it remains a strong candidate for high-concurrency web serving, software compilation pipelines, and storage-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server built on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture, featuring 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. Operating under the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 1536.0 GB of RAM alongside 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database operations, while single-core performance sits in the average tier. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for data-dense environments, offering a balanced 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, software compilation, and static web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton cores, 1536 GB RAM, 45,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: An ARM-based storage-optimized instance combining massive local NVMe capacity with high-bandwidth memory for demanding database and data-processing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
45,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
1536 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations, Redis, and multi-core processing
Best suited for large-scale databases, web serving, and software compilation
